title: Luxe Lettuce Wrapped Vietnamese Avocado Bun Cha
description: A refreshing twist on traditional Vietnamese bun cha, featuring a shio-koji-cured pork patty, crunchy avocado, and fresh herbs, all wrapped in a crisp lettuce leaf.

ingredients.name: Shio-Koji-Cured Pork Patty
ingredients.notes: See notes for shio-koji cure recipe

instructions: Prepare the shio-koji cure by mixing 1/2 cup of kosher salt, 1/4 cup of shio-koji powder, and 1/4 cup of sugar in a bowl.
Place the pork shoulder in a large ziplock bag and pour the shio-koji cure over the meat.
Massage the cure into the meat and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Grill the pork until it reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
Slice the pork into thin patties.
Cut the avocado into thin slices.
Prepare the lettuce wraps by washing and drying the leaves.
Cook the rice noodles according to the package instructions.
Assemble the bun cha by placing a pork patty on a lettuce leaf, topping with a scoop of noodles, a few slices of avocado, and a sprinkle of herbs.
Serve with a side of vinegar-based dipping sauce, chili sauce, hoisin sauce, and fresh chili slices.
